prompt: Search emails for any schedule pressure on Westfield in the last two weeks.

score.finalText:
I searched Westfield emails for schedule pressure. Nothing came back from the last two weeks.

What I did find is older closeout pressure, mostly from early April:
- **Email from Andrew Cannon on Apr 1, 2026** about **remaining items** asked for a **schedule for completion**.
- **Email from Ryan Wanninger on Apr 1, 2026** also referenced remaining site tasks — **backfilling, cutting down cleanouts, final grading** — and requested completion timing.
- **Email from Andrew Cannon on Apr 5, 2026** said **seed and straw were expected soon** and the **silt fence could be removed shortly after**, which suggests active closeout sequencing.
- There’s also a **Mar 17, 2026** email flagging **potential delays if the project is held off**, but that’s outside your two-week window.

Bottom line:
- **No email evidence of fresh schedule pressure in the last 14 days**
- The latest email signal I can see is **older closeout schedule follow-up**, not a new escalation

Data gap:
- The email search results are returning **March/early April**, so either there were **no Westfield emails in the last two weeks** or the relevant recent pressure is happening somewhere else — likely **Teams, meetings, or calls not captured in email**

Best next move:
- I should check **Teams messages and recent meetings** for Westfield schedule pressure, because that’s where the newest closeout issues usually show up.

data.message: Reading conversation memory and project context

input.query: Westfield Collective schedule pressure delay deadline milestone completion last two weeks

output.query: Westfield Collective schedule pressure delay deadline milestone completion last two weeks

results.title: Email: Re: Westfield Collective: Remaining Items
results.content: Andrew Cannon outlines the remaining tasks at the Westfield Collective site, including backfilling, cutting down cleanouts, and final grading. He requests a schedule for completion.
results.citation: Email from ["Ryan Wanninger"], Apr 1, 2026: "Email: Re: Westfield Collective: Remaining Items"

results.title: Email: Westfield Collective: 03/17/2026 OAC Update
results.content:
The document is an email update from Andrew Cannon, Assistant Project Manager at Alleato Group, addressed to Mike Ewers and Katie Conner. It serves as a communication regarding the recent progress of the Westfield Collective project, specifically following the successful opening of the building to customers on March 16, 2026. The email highlights this achievement as a significant milestone for the team and expresses enthusiasm for the positive reception from customers.

Key topics covered in the email include a congratulatory note to Mike and Katie for their efforts in welcoming customers and a mention of the OAC (Owner-Architect-Contractor) meeting that took place the previous day. Andrew indicates that Mike and Katie were unable to attend this meeting and offers to share the prepared agenda for their review. He encourages them to reach out with any questions or topics they wish to discuss further.

The email does not provide specific numbers or detailed requirements but emphasizes the importance of team collaboration and communication moving forward. As for action items, Andrew invites Mike and Katie to review the agenda from the OAC meeting and to engage in further discussion if needed, indicating a proactive approach to ensure all team members are aligned and informed about project developments.
results.citation: Email from ["Andrew Cannon"], Mar 17, 2026: "Email: Westfield Collective: 03/17/2026 OAC Update"

results.title: Email: Re: Westfield Collective: Remaining Items
results.content: Andrew Cannon provides a list of remaining items to be completed at the Westfield site and requests a schedule confirmation to wrap up the project. This serves as a final reminder for the urgency of the tasks.
results.citation: Email from ["Andrew Cannon"], Apr 1, 2026: "Email: Re: Westfield Collective: Remaining Items"

results.title: Email: Re: Westfield Collective: Remaining Items
results.content: Andrew Cannon provides an update on the Westfield Collective project, indicating that seed and straw are expected to be put down soon and that the silt fence can be removed shortly thereafter. This communication is crucial for project scheduling.
results.citation: Email from ["Andrew Cannon"], Apr 5, 2026: "Email: Re: Westfield Collective: Remaining Items"

results.title: Email: Re: Westfield Collective
results.content: risk: Potential delays if the project is held off.
results.citation: Email from ["Andrew Cannon"], Mar 17, 2026: "Email: Re: Westfield Collective"

results.title: Email: Re: Westfield Collective: 03/17/2026 OAC Update
results.content:
The document is an email correspondence regarding the update on the Westfield Collective project, specifically following its opening on March 17, 2026. The email chain includes communications between key stakeholders, including Katie Conner, the Founder and Beach Director of iBeach Volleyball, and Jesse Dawson, COO of Alleato Group, along with Andrew Cannon, Assistant Project Manager at Alleato Group. The purpose of this email is to celebrate the successful opening of the venue and to provide updates on the project's progress.

Key topics discussed in the email include the successful welcoming of customers to both sides of the Westfield Collective building, which marks a significant milestone for the project. Andrew Cannon congratulates Mike Ewers and Katie Conner for this achievement, highlighting the positive reception from the community. The email also mentions the agenda prepared for the OAC (Owner's Advisory Committee) meeting, which Katie and Mike were unable to attend, indicating ongoing project management and stakeholder engagement.

Important details include the date of the venue's opening (March 17, 2026) and the enthusiastic response from the community, as noted by Katie Conner. The email emphasizes gratitude towards the Alleato team for their support in bringing the project to completion. While no specific action items or next steps are explicitly outlined in this correspondence, the mention of the agenda for the OAC meeting suggests that further discussions and evaluations of the project's progress will be necessary among the stakeholders.
results.citation: Email from ["Katie Conner"], Mar 19, 2026: "Email: Re: Westfield Collective: 03/17/2026 OAC Update"

memories.content: The user prefers detailed, specific updates on project status, risks, and next steps for Vermillion Rise Warehouse.
User prefers high-leverage, action-oriented tasks prioritized daily for Vermillion Rise Warehouse project.
User prefers to have tasks and RFIs associated with specific project names, due dates, and assignees documented clearly.
Verify latest post-April-22 communications and check structured budget, commitments, direct costs, and change orders before quoting exposure dollars for Westfield Collective.
